rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GIEDGOWD VS. KEVIN MCGUINNESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 6, 2012, Line 1, Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs. Granted in large part. Plaintiff is awarded attorney's fees of $166,667, representing one-third of the judgment amount. While plaintiff's counsel estimates that he spent over 500 ours on this case, his reconstructed billing records show only 393.5 hours. Moreover, while plaintiff's counsel says that his hourly rate exceeds $500, he provides no information how that number was arrived at or that it has been charged to any client and he stated two years ago that his hourly rate exceeds $300. An hourly rate of $400 multiplied by 400 hours yields $160,000, which is in the vicinity of a one-third contingency fee. No costs are awarded. Recoverable costs were determined at the April 5, 2012, hearing on the motion to tax. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND B. SIMMONS VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 6, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND SIMMONS Motion To Set Aside Judgment. Denied. No judgment has been entered and plaintiff fails to state any basis for setting aside the September 1, 2011 order sustaining a demurrer to his amended complaint. Because plaintiff filed an appeal of that order, which was dismissed as premature due to lack of a judgment, the court on its own motion believes that judgment should now be entered in favor of defendant. rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $1,523.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 6, 2012, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LANA WONG VS. JOHNSON, DANIEL INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA STONE INK et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2012, line 16, DEFENDANT PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled in its entirety. The fifth and sixth causes of action are adequately pled. Even if the Court took judicial notice of the content of the CC&Rs, none of the cited sections immunizes defendant from liability. As to the seventh cause of action for violation of the Unruh Act, a condominium association is a "business establishment" under that Act. Defendant's argument that the seventh cause of action lacks the requisite ultimate facts was not considered because that argument was raised for the first time in the reply papers. While the tort of another doctrine is not a distinct cause of action but merely allows the recovery of attorney's fees as damages, there is no point in sustaining the demurrer to that alleged cause of action. The allegations in the eighth cause of action are sufficient to permit plaintiff to seek recovery of attorney's fees as damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA SANCHEZ et al VS. WILLIAM RALPH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 03, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANT WILLIAM RALPH'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is Granted with fifteen days leave to amend. The complaint does not allege sufficiently egregious conduct on the part of Mr. Ralph to warrant the award of punitive damages against him, yet it is possible that this deficiency can be cured. Plaintiffs are granted leave to allege, if they can do so in good faith, that Mr. Ralph intended to injure Plaintiffs or acted in a despicable manner that evinced a willful and conscious disregard for their rights and safety. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALI BAALOUACH VS. CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2012, line 17, DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Granted. Chicago Title is awarded attorney fees of $3000 (10 hours at $300 per hour. Chicago Title satisfied its burden of showing that non-incidental allegations of each claim arise out of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Even considering his late-filed opposition, Mr. Baalouach has not provided any evidence to satisfy his burden that there is minimal merit to either of his claims. The amount of fees sought was reduced because the hours claimed were excessive for the work reflected in the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
